content: Discussing the scope of who we want this to be for (researchers, anybody or in between) is exactly what the meta stream is for
hm. maybe we can ease into it. do you have a list of "researchers" that you could specifically send to?
I don't know, there probably isn't much risk in opening it up to people who like category theory. what do you think @Joe Moeller
Not really. Could just announce it on academic mailing lists, but I'd prefer just to throw it out on twitter and see what happens
Another way of phrasing basically the same question is "are n00b questions on-topic here?"
And I would propose yes they should be, exactly because we have streams and anyone who doesn't want to see them can just filter them out
yeah, I think most CT people nowadays welcome simple questions.
yeah, it's completely customizable for each member.
I shall throw out the invite link and see what happens
I found the problem with one of the other chats (the one with no conversation separation) to be that it was just way too chaotic.
I think group chats work really well in a certain range of number of participants.
I left like the same day. I could not handle it.
I'm still on it, there's much less activity now. But it's obvious that it isn't suitable for something like this
I really like the interface on Zulip, wrt focusing on one conversation vs seeing all conversations.
